brief_summary: Two-axis map of the four mount styles. X: feel (softer → firmer). Y: sound (deeper → higher). Gasket bottom-left, top-mount upper-right, tray-mount centered with a dashed halo indicating its high key-to-key variance, integrated upper-right corner. Bordeaux fills, qualifier captions below each label. Recommendation strip: 'pick the mount that matches the typing feel wanted, not the sound'.
data_sources: mounting-styles-compared.mdx § 'Gasket-mount' — 'gasket boards read as deeper, rounder, and more uniform ... gives perceptibly under a heavy keypress'
mounting-styles-compared.mdx § 'Top-mount' — 'tighter, more focused, and slightly higher-pitched ... feel is firmer and more direct'
mounting-styles-compared.mdx § 'Tray-mount' — 'sound is the most variable of the four styles ... keys above a standoff sound tighter and brighter, keys between standoffs sound looser and more hollow'
mounting-styles-compared.mdx § 'Integrated plate' — 'the firmest of the four ... highest-pitched and most case-dominant'
mounting-styles-compared.mdx § Closing — 'pick the mount that matches the typing feel wanted, not the sound'
